channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: J’ai transformé mon PC en développeur IA avec OpenClaw (et voici combien ça me coûte vraiment)
item.description: Je bidouille depuis vingt ans, mais je n'ai jamais su coder. Cette frustration vient d'éclater en vol. J'ai installé un agent IA autonome qui agit sur mon PC. Du jour au lendemain, je me suis retrouvé avec un développeur à demeure, capable de coder mes scripts, de gérer ma veille et même de réparer mes erreurs pendant que je dors. Récit d’une révolution intime, de ses moments de grâce jusqu'à son véritable coût. ...

item.title: Snap alums unveil Ghost Angels fund
item.description: A group of 20 Snap alumni has come together to launch a fund called Ghost Angels to back the next generation of social media.

item.title: Backrooms is a certified blockbuster with a $38 million opening day
item.description: The Kane Parsons' film Backrooms is expected to earn up to $90 million in its opening weekend after pulling down $38 million on Friday alone. That's not only above expectations, but absolutely obliterates A24's previous opening weekend record of $25.5 million for Alex Garland's Civil War. It's also a better opening day than The Mandalorian […]

item.title: Oscar-winning 'Star Wars' editor Marcia Lucas has died
item.description: Marcia Lucas, who edited 'Star Wars' and 'Return of the Jedi,' has died at 80.

item.title: ‘What a joke’: Github Copilot’s new token-based billing spurs consternation among devs
item.description: The golden age of Microsoft's Github Copilot appears to be at an end.

item.title: NASA readies the X-59 for its first supersonic flight, SpaceX's Starship grounded and more science stories
SpaceX lands a $4 billion contract to help build Trump's Golden Dome
item.description: That's on top of another $2 billion contract awarded to SpaceX earlier this week.

item.title: Welcome to Night Vale host Cecil Baldwin shares his tech pet peeves
item.description: Cecil Baldwin's résumé includes appearances on Gravity Falls, narrating the documentary Scream, Queen! My Nightmare on Elm Street, and performing as part of the New York Neo-Futurists theater company. But he is best known as the host of the podcast Welcome to Night Vale, a long-running fiction show that blends macabre Lovecraftian horror with absurdist […]...

item.title: ‘Most likely, you won’t see it on a Leica M camera’: Leica hints that generative AI tools like Gemini Omni are at odds with its photography heritage, but says they ‘make perfect sense’ for phones like the Xiaomi 17T Pro
item.description: At a roundtable attended by TechRadar, Leica shared its thoughts on the relationship between photography and generative AI.

item.title: J’ai testé l’écran motorisé ALR Nothing Projector PET Crystal Classic qui veut réconcilier vidéoprojecteur ultra courte focale et salon de vie
item.description: Pour parfaire l’image d’un vidéoprojecteur dans un salon, rien de tel qu’un écran qui s’enroule et se déroule à l’envi. J’ai pu tester pendant un petit moment le modèle PET Crystal Classic, pensé pour les modèles à ultra courte focale et distribué par Nothing Projector, et en voici mes impressions.

item.title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
item.description: Meta seems to be making big bets on AI-powered hardware.

item.title: Pourquoi cette batterie externe Ugreen de 10 000 mAh et 55 W à -33 % va plaire à tout le monde cet été ?
item.description: Avec son câble USB-C et ses deux ports de charge, cette Ugreen Nexode Pro de 55 W est un objet qui plaira à tout le monde. En ce moment, elle chute à 39,99 euros chez Amazon.

item.title: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
item.description: Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks, from inbox summaries to local event planning, but it’s unclear why Google made it a separate product.

item.title: Microsoft is threatening legal action for disclosing exploits
item.description: Microsoft is facing criticism for its handling of zero-day exploits. Someone going by the name Nightmare Eclipse has been publicly feuding with the company, posting proof-of-concept exploit code. Some of their posts suggest that they're a disgruntled former employee. But what caught cyber security researcher Kevin Beaumont's eye was how Microsoft has responded. Microsoft suggests […]...
